title: The Culture of Fear
subtitle: Why Americans Are Afraid of the Wrong Things
author: Barry Glassner
themes: Americans are manipulated into irrational fears by politicians, media, and business
Manufactured fear drives public anxiety far out of proportion to actual risk
School children are safer today than in the 1970s -- fear runs opposite to data
The economics of fear: media profits from anxiety and moral panic
Tragedy criticism substitutes for substantive political action
Prognostic fear: the art of predicting danger without evidence
Racial bias in fear amplification: black perpetrators receive more sustained attention than white
Why reduced crime rates paradoxically do not reduce fear of crime
awards: New York Times Notable Book of 1999
Named one of the best nonfiction books of 1999 by Los Angeles Times
excerpt: A provocative sociologist exposes the systematic manipulation of American anxiety by politicians, the press, and corporate interests -- and reveals that the things we fear most are rarely the things that threaten us most.
